decisions.reasoning: MSFT is down ~24% YTD (worst month since the dot-com bust) on AI capex fears: FY26 capex is tracking toward ~$190B and Q3 FCF fell 10% even as Azure grew 40% and revenue beat (+18.3% YoY). This is a known, already-priced-in structural repricing rather than a fresh negative catalyst today, so selling now would lock in a loss without new information. No prior journal thesis exists (first session) to reverse. SMA trend is bearish (fast 372 < slow 400) which argues against adding, so holding the existing 3 shares flat is the balanced call.

sources.headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Fell on Heavy AI Capex and Softer Azure Growth
Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Outlook for 2026 | June Edition - cheapest forward multiple since 2023

decisions.invalidation: A guidance cut on Azure growth or capex ROI at the next earnings print, or a credible report that AI infrastructure demand is being canceled (not just delayed), would justify trimming.

decisions.reasoning: Michael Burry's public short thesis (hyperscaler demand concentration, $662B in off-balance-sheet data-center lease commitments per a Feb 2026 Moody's report, Cisco-dot-com comparison) is a real, well-argued bear case, but the sell-side consensus remains 'Strong Buy' with a $299 target (~50% upside), and today's macro tape shows chip stocks bouncing on rate relief rather than a company-specific negative. Next earnings isn't until Aug 26, so there's no fresh catalyst today to act on either direction. Holding the existing 15 shares avoids both panic-selling into a known thesis and FOMO-buying into a macro-driven green day.

sources.headline: Michael Burry Bets Nvidia Peak May Be Near as Numbers Back His Case
NVIDIA Price Forecast: Burry Shorts NVDA, but Analysts See $299 Upside

decisions.invalidation: A hyperscaler (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN/META) publicly cutting capex guidance, or NVDA guiding down at the Aug 26 print, would validate the demand-concentration thesis and trigger a trim.

decisions.reasoning: QQQ signal is flat and the position is a small -1.98% unrealized loss with no stock-specific catalyst; today's move is macro (cooler June jobs print: +57K vs +113K expected, unemployment 4.2% vs 4.3% expected, per CNBC July 2 coverage) pushing the Nasdaq +0.7% on rate-hike relief. A broad market pop on macro data isn't a reason to add or trim a diversified index holding.

sources.headline: Stock futures gain as chip stocks bounce, weak jobs report eases interest rate pressure
Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq rise after June jobs report falls short

decisions.invalidation: A hawkish repricing (hot CPI or a hawkish FOMC surprise at the July 29 meeting) that reverses today's rate-relief rally.

decisions.reasoning: SPY is the only position currently green (+2.3%) and signal is flat; the June jobs miss (57K vs 113K expected) supported a Fed-on-hold narrative and pushed the S&P to a new intraday high today per Yahoo Finance. This is a broad-market macro move, not an SPY-specific catalyst, so no action beyond holding is warranted; adding now would be chasing a same-day rally.

sources.headline: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq rise after June jobs report falls short

decisions.invalidation: A reversal on hotter-than-expected CPI or a hawkish Fed signal ahead of the July 29 FOMC meeting.

summary: First journal session (no prior context); held all four existing positions (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY) as today's move was a macro rate-relief rally on a cooler June jobs report rather than a stock-specific catalyst, and MSFT/NVDA's known AI-capex and Burry short overhangs are already priced in rather than fresh negatives.
open_theses: MSFT: AI capex spend vs. Azure growth trade-off remains the key swing factor; watch next earnings for capex ROI signal.
NVDA: Burry's hyperscaler-demand-concentration short thesis vs. Street's $299 target; watch for any hyperscaler capex guidance cuts before the Aug 26 NVDA print.
Macro: July 29 FOMC meeting is the next major catalyst after today's cooler jobs report; CPI print before then could swing rate expectations again.
